tasks.title: Move login lockout state to a shared backend (Redis) instead of in-process memory
tasks.problem: Lockout counters are still process-local. In multi-worker or multi-pod deployments, attackers can bypass lockout by spraying requests across instances, and all lockout state is lost on restart.

evidence.note: Login cooldown checks read directly from those in-process stores.

actions: Implement a Redis-backed `LockoutStore` (sliding-window counters + TTL lock key), selectable via config.
Add integration tests proving lockout consistency across two app instances sharing Redis and persistence across process restart.
acceptance_criteria: Lockout behavior is identical regardless of which app worker handles each request.
Restarting a worker does not immediately clear active lockouts.
Local/test mode still runs without Redis.

tasks.title: Remove account-state enumeration signals from `/api/auth/login`
tasks.problem: `/login` returns distinguishable responses for invalid credentials vs. Google-only account vs. unverified/deactivated account. This leaks account existence and account type to unauthenticated callers.

evidence.note: Unknown user returns `401 Invalid email or password`.
Google-auth account returns `400 This account uses Google sign-in`.

actions: Normalize externally visible failure response for all credential/account-state failures to a single 401 message.
Log internal reason codes server-side for support/debugging (`bad_password`, `google_only`, `unverified`, `inactive`).
Add regression tests asserting response-body/status equivalence across all failed-login variants.
acceptance_criteria: Unauthenticated callers cannot infer whether an email exists, is Google-only, unverified, or deactivated from login responses.
Operational logs still preserve enough reason detail for incident triage.

tasks.title: Serialize startup migrations with a PostgreSQL advisory lock
tasks.problem: Every app instance runs `apply_pending()` during startup, but migration execution is not guarded by a cross-process lock. Concurrent startups can race on DDL and produce avoidable startup failures.

evidence.note: Migrations are run in app lifespan at startup for every instance.
`apply_pending` has no advisory lock / inter-process serialization.

actions: Wrap migration execution in `pg_advisory_lock` (or `pg_try_advisory_lock` with timeout + clear error).
Hold the lock for the full validation+apply phase and release in `finally`.
Add a concurrency regression test with two connections attempting migration apply in parallel.
acceptance_criteria: Parallel app startups no longer race each other while applying migrations.
If lock acquisition times out, startup fails with a deterministic operator-facing error.

tasks.title: Optimize prediction bulk insert path for large scoring batches
tasks.problem: `insert_predictions_batch` uses `cursor.executemany` on an autocommit connection, which scales poorly for large batches because it executes many individual INSERT statements.

evidence.note: Batch insert currently uses `executemany` with one-row VALUES template.

actions: Switch to `psycopg2.extras.execute_values` (paged multi-row insert) and explicit transaction for the batch.
Add a performance regression benchmark test (or scripted check) for 1k+ prediction rows.
acceptance_criteria: Large scoring batches complete with materially lower DB round-trip overhead.
No behavior change in conflict handling or persisted payload shape.
